exemplar.title: develop a website highlighting unique and unknown sports around the world

detailedExemplarData.title: develop a website highlighting unique and unknown sports around the world

strengths: Well-demonstrated information literacy with effective cross-referencing and source evaluation
Use of a Gantt chart shows strong organizational skills supporting timely task completion
Visuals and concise text exhibit formal yet accessible communication, engaging the target audience
Innovative implementation of swipe effects and interactive design enhances user experience
weaknesses: Practices are not explicitly linked to the MYP ATL skill categories to clarify alignment
feedback.calculationMethod:
Mini report holistic evaluation: **Strengths**
â¢ The project shows **strong information literacy**, with clear evidence of source evaluation and cross-referencing that underpin the contentâs credibility.
â¢ Incorporating a **Gantt chart** demonstrates excellent self-management and planning, ensuring tasks are sequenced and deadlines are met.
â¢ The balance of **visuals and concise language** reflects thoughtful communication strategies, making the website both professional and user-friendly.
â¢ Creative interactive elements, such as swipe effects, highlight **innovative thinking** and enhance the overall user experience.

**Areas for Improvement**
â¢ Explicitly map each practice to the relevant MYP ATL skill (e.g., research, self-management, communication) to **demonstrate clear alignment** with the ATL framework and show how these skills drove both learning and product quality.

strengths: Clearly articulated learning goal linking cultural diversity and sustainability with a focused website aim
Effective use of personal anecdotes to justify and ground the learning goal in real experiences
weaknesses: Success criteria lack measurable targets for objective evaluation
Redundancy in the description of globalization and sustainability weakens focus
Insufficient elaboration on how the âIdentities and relationshipsâ context shapes page content
Embedded evaluation rubrics are too generic and not tailored to website objectives
Action plan lacks detailed sequencing, clear dependencies, and quantified milestones
Responsibilities and required resources are not specified for key tasks

strengths: Insightful reflection on personal growth, linking project challenges to increased confidence and career aspirations
Critical evaluation of mobile layout distortion shows an ability to identify and analyze design issues
Incorporation of peer feedback demonstrates proactive engagement and openness to improving navigation
weaknesses: Reflections on skill development are overly detailed and need focusing on key evidence tied to success criteria
Evaluation of lower-rated areas lacks depth, particularly on how feedback informs future improvements
Limited use of concrete data (analytics or survey results) to substantiate evaluation against success criteria
feedback.calculationMethod:
Mini report holistic evaluation: **Strengths**
â¢ The student offers **meaningful insights** into how overcoming project challenges has bolstered confidence and informed future career goals in sports and web design.
â¢ Acknowledging the **mobile layout distortion** reflects a high level of critical self-evaluation and attention to technical detail.
â¢ By describing the peer-suggested search bar feature, the reflection shows **active responsiveness** to user feedback, indicating a learner-centered approach.

**Areas for Improvement**
â¢ Streamline reflections on skill development to focus on the most **pivotal evidence** and explicitly link this growth to the defined success criteria.
â¢ Provide a deeper analysis of areas where performance was lower, detailing **specific steps** for improvement based on feedback.
â¢ Incorporate **concrete evidence**âsuch as website analytics, user engagement data, or survey resultsâto robustly evaluate how each success criterion was met.

annotations.comment: Your final product specifications identify key aspects, but success criteria lack measurable targets (e.g., specific engagement rates or error thresholds) to evaluate achievement objectively.
Your learning goal is clearly articulated, linking cultural diversity and sustainability with a defined website aim, reflecting strong personal interest and a focused objective.
Your personal anecdotes in physical education effectively justify the learning goal; consider clarifying how each experience will inform specific website features.
The description of globalization and sustainability is repeated; consider consolidating these paragraphs to sharpen focus and avoid redundancy.
Your chosen global context âIdentities and relationshipsâ is relevant, yet further elaboration on how it directly shapes your website content and criteria would enhance planning clarity.
The embedded generic evaluation rubrics appear standard rather than tailored; refine to focus on criteria specific to your websiteâs objectives.
The action plan outlines steps and timelines but could benefit from more detailed sequencing, clear dependencies, and quantified milestones to track progress effectively.
While your action plan lists tasks comprehensively, some steps lack clear responsible parties and required resources; specifying these details will improve feasibility.
Your use of information literacy is well exemplified with cross-referencing and source evaluation, demonstrating effective ATL application to support learning.
You articulate information literacy well, but explicitly linking these practices to defined MYP ATL skill categories (e.g., self-management, research) would clarify alignment with ATL frameworks.
Integrating a Gantt chart to structure tasks shows strong organizational skill, directly supporting timely achievement of your learning and product goals.
Embedding formal yet accessible communication through visuals and concise text effectively engages users and conveys content clearly.
Your creative use of swipe effects and interactive design demonstrates innovative thinking, enhancing user experience and reflecting strong ATL application.
You reflect insightfully on personal growth, linking project challenges to enhanced confidence in public speaking and career aspirations in sports and web design.
Your reflection on skill development is comprehensive but could be more concise, focusing on pivotal evidence of growth and linking to specific success criteria.
Self-evaluation applies success rubrics well, but consider deeper analysis of areas rated lower (e.g., engagement levels), including how feedback will inform future improvements.
Acknowledging the mobile layout distortion in reflection shows critical evaluation; consider specifying responsive adjustments to address this in future iterations.
Incorporating peer feedback (search bar suggestion) illustrates proactive reflection on navigation; outlining next steps would strengthen your evaluation.
Your descriptions of project impact are strong; deepen reflection by evaluating how each success criterion was met, using concrete evidence from your website analytics or survey data.
